meta.name: K10 Kessler Psychological Distress Scale
meta.description: The K10 is a 10-item questionnaire designed to measure non-specific psychological distress. It assesses the level of anxiety and depressive symptoms a person has experienced in the most recent 4-week period.
meta.author: Kessler, Andrews, Colpe et al. (2002)
meta.reference: Kessler RC, Andrews G, Colpe LJ, et al. Short screening scales to monitor population prevalences and trends in non-specific psychological distress. Psychol Med. 2002;32(6):959-976.
meta.instruction: The following questions ask about how you have been feeling during the past 4 weeks. For each question, select the option that best describes how often you had this feeling.

questions.text: In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel tired out for no good reason?
In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel nervous?
In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel so nervous that nothing could calm you down?
In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel hopeless?
In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel restless or fidgety?
In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel so restless you could not sit still?
In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel depressed?
In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel that everything was an effort?
In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel so sad that nothing could cheer you up?
In the past 4 weeks, about how often did you feel worthless?

ranges.label: Likely to Be Well
ranges.description: Your score suggests you are likely to be well with no significant psychological distress.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current healthy coping strategies
Keep up regular physical activity and social engagement
Practice stress management techniques as a preventive measure

ranges.label: Mild Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates mild levels of psychological distress. You may be experiencing some emotional difficulties.
suggestions: Monitor your emotional state over the coming weeks
Practice relaxation techniques such as deep breathing or mindfulness
Talk to a trusted friend or family member about how you feel
Consider consulting a healthcare professional if symptoms persist

ranges.label: Moderate Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate levels of psychological distress that may be affecting your daily functioning.
suggestions: Consider seeking help from a mental health professional
Maintain regular routines for sleep, exercise, and nutrition
Reduce stressors where possible and prioritize self-care
Avoid using alcohol or drugs to cope with distress

ranges.label: Severe Psychological Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates severe psychological distress. It is strongly recommended that you seek professional help.
suggestions: Please consult a mental health professional or your doctor as soon as possible
If you are in crisis, contact a crisis helpline (e.g., 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline)
Do not hesitate to reach out to trusted people in your life
Follow through with professional treatment recommendations
